DECLARATION OF MR. RICHARD LAWSON IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT THE LAWSON ROOFING CO., INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND DISMISS OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, STAY PROCEEDINGS (TRANSACTION ID # 76489957) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE LAWSON ROOFING CO., INC.

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE SEP-30-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE LAWSON ROOFING CO., INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT THE LAWSON ROOFING CO. MOVES TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND SEEKS SANCTIONS FOR PLAINTIFF FERNANDO MONTANO'S FAILURE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE APPLICABILITY OF THE COLLECTIVE BARGAINING AGREEMENT (CBA) THAT LAWSON CONTENDS REQUIRES ARBITRATION. FOR THE REASONS STATED HEREIN, THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS BUT DENIES SANCTIONS (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Sep-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT THE LAWSON ROOFING CO., INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant the Lawson Roofing Co. moves to compel arbitration and seeks sanctions for plaintiff Fernando Montano's failure to meet and confer regarding the applicability of the collective bargaining agreement (CBA) that Lawson contends requires arbitration. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings but denies sanctions (see word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
